sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: HERBST GROUP, LLC's JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF(JEPI) Holding History
feed.summary: This article details HERBST GROUP, LLC's historical holdings of the JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPI). As of December 31, 2025, HERBST GROUP, LLC held 35,251 shares valued at $2.02 million, representing 1.19% of their portfolio. The firm has made 8 purchases and 6 sales of JEPI since Q3 2022, and currently has an estimated gain of $31,860.80 on these holdings.

feed.title: CoStar Group stock hits 52-week low at 38.67 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: CoStar Group (CSGP) stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $38.67, representing a 46% decrease over the past year due to market volatility and sector pressures. Despite the downturn, InvestingPro suggests the stock may be undervalued. Recent company developments include an expansion of Homes.com, a new board member, and the CEO's significant share purchase, which analysts view as a vote of confidence.

feed.title: There are real savings hiding in Home Depot’s Spring Black Friday sale
feed.summary: The Home Depot's Spring Black Friday sale is currently underway, offering discounts on over 1,000 items such as grills, lawn mowers, patio sets, and major appliances until April 29th. The article highlights specific deals including a Weber Genesis E-315 Gas Grill, a DEWALT self-propelled lawn mower, and a Halmuz 7-Piece Patio Furniture Set. It also addresses frequently asked questions regarding free shipping, appliance bundles, and the types of gear included in the sale.

feed.title: How Investors May Respond To TJX (TJX) Dividend Hike And New Share Repurchase Plan
feed.summary: TJX Companies announced a 13% increase in its quarterly dividend to US$0.48 per share and a new US$2.75 billion share repurchase authorization for Fiscal Year 2027. This move emphasizes the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders and reinforces confidence in its business model, supported by recent earnings growth and solid margins. However, investors should also consider potential risks such as rising labor costs and increased competition from e-commerce before making investment decisions.

feed.title: Texas Instruments (TXN) Stock Is Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) shares rose 2.7% after Stifel Nicolaus upgraded the stock to a "Buy" with a $250 price target, citing the company reaching a turning point where heavy investments in new factories are expected to pay off. Analysts predict increased cash generation and margin growth as the building phase concludes and the company sells more specialized chips. This news follows a previous uptick due to a new government initiative aimed at securing semiconductor supply chains.

feed.title: TCOM Stockholders Have Rights – If You Lost Money Investing
feed.summary: Robbins LLP is reminding stockholders of Trip.com Group Limited (NASDAQ: TCOM) that a class action lawsuit has been filed on behalf of investors who purchased shares between April 30, 2025, and January 13, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Trip.com Group understated the regulatory risks associated with its monopolistic business practices, leading to a significant drop in stock price after news of an antitrust probe by China's State Administration for Market Regulation became public. Shareholders have until May 11, 2026, to submit their papers to the court to serve as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Corning Stock Surges Past $170 on AI Fiber Optics Boom and Upgraded Growth Targets
feed.summary: Corning Inc. shares surged over 3% to trade around $170.30, driven by its critical role in providing high-density optical fiber solutions for AI data centers and hyperscale demand. The company has delivered approximately 89% year-to-date gains in 2026, solidifying its position in the tech supply chain amid accelerating AI infrastructure spending. Corning also announced a multiyear agreement with Meta Platforms valued at up to $6 billion and upgraded its Springboard growth plan, targeting an additional $11 billion in incremental annualized sales by the end of 2028.

feed.title: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods Raises Stock Yards Bancorp (NASDAQ:SYBT) Price Target to $80.00
feed.summary: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods has raised its price target for Stock Yards Bancorp (NASDAQ:SYBT) from $79.00 to $80.00, maintaining a "market perform" rating. This new target suggests a potential upside of 12.08% from the current stock price. Analyst consensus on SYBT is generally a "Hold" with an average price target of $77.75, although some firms like Hovde Group have recently upgraded the stock to "Outperform."

feed.title: Autodesk Inc Stock (ADSK) Moved Down by 8.52% on Apr 9: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Autodesk Inc. (ADSK) stock fell by 8.52% due to a combination of accounting concerns, a valuation reset, and broader risk-off sentiment in the software sector. Analysts have reduced price targets for ADSK, influenced by past regulatory inquiries into the company's financial practices and an ongoing class action lawsuit regarding free cash flow metrics. Despite strong recent financial performance, these company-specific issues, along with macro factors, are contributing to investor caution.

feed.title: Autozone Inc Stock (AZO) Moved Up by 3.27% on Apr 9: Key Drivers Unveiled
feed.summary: AutoZone (AZO) stock rose 3.27% on April 9th, driven by a recent analyst upgrade and strong industry fundamentals. The company benefits from the increasing age of vehicles, boosting demand for auto parts. Despite some Q2 2026 financial challenges like margin compression and a revenue miss, analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating with positive future earnings projections.

feed.title: Exxon Mobil (XOM) Stock Reverses As Oil Rally Loses Steam
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil's stock initially rose due to Middle East tensions causing an oil price jump but reversed course as oil pared gains and market sentiment shifted away from an energy crisis. Despite the intraday pullback, the stock maintains a "Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $151.76. Threats to the Strait of Hormuz initially supported oil, but hopes of de-escalation between Lebanon and Israel cooled crude prices, leading to XOM's reversal.

feed.title: Ford removing Carroll Shelby’s name from streets near new HQ
feed.summary: Ford is reportedly removing Carroll Shelby's name from streets near its new headquarters, instead choosing to honor the Raptor. This move marks a change in how the company recognizes automotive figures in areas surrounding its facilities. The article highlights this decision without providing further details on the specific streets or reasons.

feed.title: IT Stockholders Have Rights – If You Lost Money Investing
feed.summary: Robbins LLP is reminding investors who purchased Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT) common stock between February 4, 2025, and February 2, 2026, about a class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Gartner misled investors regarding its growth and projected revenue, particularly concerning contract value (CV) growth rates and its Consulting segment's performance. The full truth reportedly emerged on February 3, 2026, leading to a significant drop in Gartner's stock price.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has filed a preliminary pricing supplement for "Auto Callable Contingent Interest Notes" linked to the Russell 2000 and S&P 500 Indices, fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. These notes are designed for investors seeking contingent interest payments if both indices meet specific performance criteria, with automatic early call features. Investing in these notes involves significant risks, including the potential loss of principal and dependence on the creditworthiness of both JPMorgan Financial and JPMorgan Chase & Co.

feed.title: HOTH.O PE Ratio & Valuation, Is HOTH.O Overvalued
feed.summary: This article provides a valuation analysis of Hoth Therapeutics Inc (HOTH.O), indicating that the stock is currently in the "Fair zone" based on its forward PS ratio compared to its five-year average. It details various valuation metrics, including Price-to-Book (P/B) ratio and Forward Free Cash Flow (FCF) yield, and compares its P/S ratio to competitors. The analysis suggests that while the stock is fairly valued, its premium given zero revenue growth may be unsustainable.

feed.title: Form FWP TORONTO DOMINION BANK Filed by: TORONTO DOMINION BANK
feed.summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank has filed a Form FWP for Market Linked Securities – Auto-Callable with Contingent Coupon and Contingent Downside Principal at Risk Securities, linked to the common stock of Broadcom Inc., due April 26, 2029. These securities offer contingent coupon payments and automatic call features, but investors face risks including potential loss of principal, no fixed interest payments, and full downside exposure to the underlying stock. The estimated value of the securities is expected to be less than the public offering price.

feed.title: Scotiabank Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for TFI International (TSE:TFII) Stock
feed.summary: Scotiabank has raised its price target for TFI International (TSE:TFII) to C$170.00, reiterating a "sector perform" rating. Despite this, the target implies a slight downside from the stock's current price, though other analysts like Desjardins and National Bank also have increased targets and "buy" or "outperform" ratings. The company recently reported C$1.50 EPS and C$2.51 billion in revenue for the quarter, with analysts forecasting C$12.23 EPS for the fiscal year.

feed.title: Former Jefferson CEO Stephen Klasko to step down as Teleflex chairman, take new health care role
feed.summary: Dr. Stephen Klasko, former CEO of Jefferson Health, is stepping down as chairman of Wayne-based Teleflex. The departure comes amidst pressure from an activist investor pushing for board changes, including Klasko's removal. He is expected to take on a new role in the health care sector.

feed.title: Telos Xacta cyber platform achieves FedRAMP High authorization
feed.summary: Telos Corporation announced that its Xacta Cyber GRC platform, including the Xacta.io and Xacta.ai modules, has achieved full FedRAMP Impact Level High authorization. This authorization allows federal agencies to use the platform for highly sensitive unclassified information. Despite mixed stock performance, analysts predict profitability for Telos this year, with InvestingPro indicating the stock is undervalued.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma Inc stock: What you should know before investing now
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma Inc continues to lead in premium home furnishings, appealing to affluent shoppers globally through brands like Pottery Barn and West Elm. The article delves into its business strengths, market position, financial highlights including a solid dividend policy, and analyst views. It also addresses potential risks like macroeconomic pressures and supply chain disruptions, advising investors on what to monitor.

feed.title: Harrah’s Oklahoma Officially Opens in Chandler
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment has officially opened Harrah’s Oklahoma in Chandler, its first managed property in the state, in partnership with the Iowa Tribe of Oklahoma. The new casino features 12 table games, over 1,000 slot machines, dining options, and is expected to create more than 300 jobs, boosting economic development and tourism along the Route 66 corridor. The grand opening included appearances by Oklahoma football legends Barry Switzer and Bob Stoops, live entertainment, and fireworks.

feed.title: Fastenal Co stock: Steady performer in industrial distribution – what you need to know
feed.summary: Fastenal Co is highlighted as a reliable industrial distributor specializing in fasteners, tools, and safety gear, with a robust distribution model and focus on efficiency. The article emphasizes its defensive growth characteristics, strong competitive position through vending technology, and financial discipline, making it an attractive long-term investment despite potential risks from industrial slowdowns.

feed.title: Best Fertilizer Stocks Worth Watching - April 9th
feed.summary: MarketBeat identifies CF Industries (CF), Nutrien (NTR), and Mosaic (MOS) as top fertilizer stocks to watch due to high recent trading volume and potential upside from the geopolitical situation around the Hormuz closure. These companies are sensitive to agricultural demand, commodity prices, and seasonal factors. CF focuses on nitrogen, Nutrien is an integrated crop-inputs provider, and Mosaic specializes in phosphate and potash.

feed.title: State Street (STT): Keefe, Bruyette & Woods Raises Price Target
feed.summary: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods has maintained an "Outperform" rating on State Street (STT) and increased its price target from $147.00 to $157.00. This adjustment reflects an positive outlook for the company, which is a key player in financial services with significant assets under custody and management. Despite this, GuruFocus estimates suggest a potential downside based on its GF Value calculation.

feed.title: Telos Corporation’s Xacta Platform Achieves Full FedRAMP High Authorization with Addition of Xacta.io and Xacta.ai
feed.summary: Telos Corporation's Xacta Cyber GRC platform, including the new Xacta.io and Xacta.ai modules, has achieved full FedRAMP Impact Level High authorization. This authorization signifies that the platform meets the stringent security requirements for operating in sensitive government environments, enhancing continuous monitoring and AI-driven compliance. The expanded Xacta suite aims to reduce manual effort, improve risk visibility, and streamline audit readiness for federal agencies.

feed.title: Telos Corporation’s Xacta Platform Achieves Full FedRAMP High Authorization with Addition of Xacta.io and Xacta.ai
feed.summary: Telos (NASDAQ: TLS) announced that its Xacta Cyber GRC platform, including the Xacta.io and Xacta.ai modules, has achieved full FedRAMP Impact Level High authorization. This expansion builds on the July 2025 Xacta 360 High approval, enabling continuous monitoring and AI-driven compliance for sensitive federal environments and streamlining audit readiness. The authorization allows federal agencies to handle highly sensitive unclassified data like PII and healthcare records securely.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Offers Carriers Relief as Diesel Prices Surge
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson is providing temporary financial relief to its carrier network by waiving fees on its discount fuel card and fuel-related cash advances for April and May. This initiative aims to help carriers, especially smaller businesses and owner-operators, offset the significant increase in diesel costs driven by current geopolitical instability. The program offers potential savings of up to $9,000 annually per truck and allows carriers to access up to 60% of their load pay as cash advances.

feed.title: Report: Walt Disney Co. Plans Up to 1,000 Layoffs
feed.summary: Walt Disney Co. is reportedly planning to lay off up to 1,000 employees in the coming weeks as part of cost-cutting measures under new CEO Josh D’Amaro. These layoffs are primarily expected to impact Disney's recently consolidated marketing division. The company, like others in entertainment, is facing challenges with lower streaming profits, weaker box office returns, and increased competition.

feed.title: SNTI: SENTI-202 achieved 50% ORR and 42% CR/CRh with excellent safety in R/R AML Phase 1 trial
feed.summary: SENTI-202, an off-the-shelf logic-gated CAR-NK cell therapy developed by Senti Biosciences (SNTI), demonstrated significant efficacy in a Phase 1 trial for high-risk relapsed/refractory Acute Myeloid Leukemia (R/R AML) patients. The therapy achieved a 50% overall response rate (ORR) and a 42% complete remission/complete remission with partial hematologic recovery (CR/CRh), with all CRs being MRD-negative, alongside strong safety. Senti Biosciences plans a pivotal trial for 2026 and intends to expand the platform into solid tumors.

feed.title: SAIC Appoints AI Expert Paul Eremenko and Cybersecurity Leader Admiral Mike Rogers to Board of Directors
feed.summary: SAIC has appointed AI expert Paul Eremenko and cybersecurity leader Admiral Mike Rogers to its Board of Directors, expanding the board from 11 to 13 members. Both new appointees will serve on the Audit Committee and Technology Committee. Eremenko brings expertise in AI and advanced technology, while Adm. Rogers offers extensive experience in cybersecurity, intelligence, and defense technology.

feed.title: The ALPS SDOG ETF: Value, Dividends, and More
feed.summary: The ALPS Sector Dividend Dogs ETF (SDOG) is highlighted as a high-dividend and value asset with considerable momentum. The article discusses SDOG's strategy, which applies the "Dogs of the Dow Theory" on a sector-by-sector basis using the S&P 500, emphasizing high dividend exposure and an equal-weight methodology to reduce sector risk. It suggests that value stocks, like those held by SDOG, are poised for a continued rebound and can complement growth funds for a more resilient portfolio.

feed.title: SailPoint appoints Levent Besik as Chief Product Officer
feed.summary: SailPoint has appointed Levent Besik as its new Chief Product Officer (CPO) to lead innovation in identity security, particularly in the context of agentic AI. Besik brings extensive experience from Microsoft, Okta, and Google, where he held significant product leadership roles in identity and AI platforms. His appointment is expected to strengthen SailPoint's position as a leader in adaptive identity security solutions for enterprises.

feed.title: Canada's RBC plans to deploy up to C$1 billion to support homegrown companies
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) announced plans to deploy up to C$1 billion over the coming years to support homegrown Canadian companies with a growth fund and equity investments. CEO Dave McKay stated this initiative aims to shift the narrative of Canadian entrepreneurs seeking foreign capital and often leaving their home market. RBC will focus on helping companies build and scale in Canada, provide lending and advisory services for major projects, and expand into sectors like defense and infrastructure.

feed.title: Director at Broadridge (NYSE: BR) adds 127 deferred stock units
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions director Robert N. Duelks was granted 127 deferred stock units of common stock at no cost under the company's 2018 Omnibus Award Plan. These units vest immediately and will convert to shares upon his departure from board service, increasing his direct holdings to 20,942 shares. This grant was issued in connection with the regular quarterly dividend, as detailed in a Form 4 SEC filing.

feed.title: Amex To Open Two New Lounges—Here Are The Best Credit Cards To Get You Into Centurion Lounges
feed.summary: American Express announced plans for two new lounges in Boston and Charlotte, along with an expansion at Dallas Fort Worth. Access to these Centurion Lounges is exclusively through specific Amex credit cards, such as the American Express Platinum Card® and The Business Platinum Card® from American Express, which carry high annual fees but include numerous travel perks. The new locations aim to enhance the travel experience for cardholders with amenities like food, Wi-Fi, and in some cases, shower suites and family rooms.

feed.title: McKesson CFO Transition Puts Capital Allocation And Valuation In Focus
feed.summary: McKesson (NYSE:MCK) announced the retirement of EVP and CFO Britt Vitalone, with Kenny Cheung set to take over the role on May 29, 2026. This leadership change will shift the responsibility for McKesson's financial decisions and capital allocation to the new CFO. Investors are encouraged to monitor changes in company communication, guidance, and balance sheet strategies as Cheung assumes his new position to assess potential impacts on the stock's future performance.

feed.title: Costco Speeds Checkout And Tests Buzzworthy Products As Valuation Stays Rich
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale is implementing automated pay stations to enhance checkout efficiency and throughput, particularly during peak hours. The company is also seeing high demand for unique products like gold bars and premium non-alcoholic mixes, demonstrating its strategy to test and scale buzzworthy offerings. These operational and product innovations aim to refine Costco's value proposition and maintain member engagement, though its valuation remains rich according to Simply Wall St's analysis.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Joins NVIDIA To Target Humanoid Robots And AI Infrastructure
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) has partnered with NVIDIA to advance humanoid robot integration, focusing on sensor fusion and real-time control, and expanding AI robotics capabilities with growing exposure to data center applications. This collaboration positions TXN's hardware as a standard for AI-powered industrial systems and humanoid robots, validating Wall Street's conviction in TXN's post-investment cycle for AI-driven demand. Investors should monitor how quickly this partnership translates into design wins and impacts TXN's product mix and capital allocation.

feed.title: Is Synchrony (SYF) Quietly Redefining Health Financing With Its Walmart.com CareCredit Expansion?
feed.summary: Synchrony (SYF) recently announced the expansion of its CareCredit card acceptance to Walmart.com, allowing cardholders to use it for medical supplies, fitness equipment, sleep products, and pet care. This move, coupled with financial literacy grants, aims to broaden Synchrony's reach in health and wellness financing. While supporting growth in health and wellness receivables, the expansion doesn't fully address key risks like partner concentration and emerging payment models.

feed.title: Royal Bank of Canada announces election of directors
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada announced that the nominees listed in its Management Proxy Circular dated February 10, 2026, were elected as directors at the Annual Meeting of Common Shareholders. The detailed voting results for each of the 13 elected directors are provided, showing high percentages of votes in favor. Final voting results for all matters will be made available on the RBC website and filed with securities regulators.

feed.title: Texas Pacific Land stock tumbles 16% after board member dies
feed.summary: Texas Pacific Land Corporation (NYSE:TPL) saw its stock tumble 16% after announcing the death of board member Murray Stahl. Stahl also served as CEO and chief investment strategist of Horizon Kinetics, Texas Pacific Land's largest shareholder, leading to concerns about leadership transition. LandBridge (NYSE:LB) and Horizon Kinetics (NYSE:HKHC) also experienced declines after the news.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum Just Discovered Oil in an Exploratory Well Off the Gulf of America. Here Are 2 More Stocks That Could Soar as a Result.
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum recently announced a significant oil discovery at its Bandit site in the Gulf of America, holding over 45% stake in the project. This find is crucial as the Gulf region accounts for about 15% of U.S. oil production and the well could potentially yield 230 million barrels of oil equivalent. Chevron and Woodside Energy Group, who are also stakeholders in the Bandit prospect, stand to benefit alongside Occidental Petroleum from this discovery and the rising oil prices.

feed.title: Starbucks (SBUX) Steals Another Executive from Chipotle. This Time to Improve Its Brand Design
feed.summary: Starbucks has hired Stephen Piacentini from Chipotle Mexican Grill to become its new coffee house design and development officer, aiming to revive its brand design. This move is part of CEO Brian Niccol's "Back to Starbucks" turnaround strategy, which also involved hiring Niccol himself from Chipotle in 2024 and several other former Chipotle executives. The company plans significant expansion in new retail locations in fiscal 2026.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider General Electric (GE) After A 65% One-Year Surge?
feed.summary: General Electric (GE) has seen significant gains over the past few years, leading investors to question its current valuation. While a Discounted Cash Flow analysis suggests GE is "about right" in its current pricing at around US$280.47 compared to its market price of US$308, the article cautions that valuation can change rapidly. The price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio also aligns with industry averages, indicating fair valuation based on current fundamentals, but two "narratives" are presented for investors to consider a bull case (fair value US$357.24) and a bear case (fair value US$296.86).

feed.title: Broadridge (NYSE: BR) director receives dividend-linked stock unit awards
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions director Annette L. Nazareth received two awards of common stock (Deferred Stock Units) totaling 33 shares, reported on April 8, 2026. These units, granted at $0.00 per share, are dividend-linked and vest upon grant, settling in shares when she separates from service. Following these awards, Nazareth directly holds 9,369 shares of Broadridge common stock.

feed.title: Broadridge (BR) director Eileen Murray granted new deferred stock unit awards
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions director Eileen K. Murray has received new deferred stock unit awards, totaling 29 units, tied to the company's regular quarterly dividend and her participation in the Director Deferred Compensation Program. These units vest immediately but will settle in Broadridge common shares upon her separation from service. Following these grants, her directly held common stock position, as reported in this filing, is 5,275 shares.

feed.title: Broadridge (BR) director Brett Keller receives additional deferred stock unit awards
feed.summary: Broadridge (BR) director Brett Keller was granted additional Deferred Stock Units (DSUs) as compensation, totaling 80 shares (54 and 26 units). These awards are tied to the company's regular quarterly dividend and were issued under the 2018 Omnibus Award Plan and Director Deferred Compensation Program. The DSUs vest immediately but will only settle in shares upon Keller's separation from service, reflecting routine compensation rather than an open-market transaction.

feed.title: Tesla's lower-cost EV plan seen boosting volume, risking margins
feed.summary: Tesla's plan to develop a smaller, lower-cost EV is expected to boost sales and address slowing demand, especially in competitive markets like China. However, investors and analysts warn that this strategy could further strain Tesla's already pressured profit margins. The move comes as Tesla faces increasing competition and weakening demand, indicated by a significant gap between production and deliveries.

feed.title: HII Hosts PAE Maritime Christopher Miller at Ingalls Shipbuilding
feed.summary: HII hosted Christopher Miller, the U.S. Navy’s portfolio acquisition executive for maritime (PAE Maritime), at its Ingalls Shipbuilding division. During his visit, Miller received updates on current ship programs, facility investments, and Ingalls' expanding production capacity. The visit highlighted HII's commitment to strengthening the Navy's fleet and the critical role of the shipbuilding industry in national defense.

feed.title: KLA Corp Stock (KLAC) Moved Up by 3.62% on Apr 9: Key Drivers Unveiled
feed.summary: KLA Corp (KLAC) stock rose by 3.62% today, driven by strong growth in the semiconductor equipment market, strategic company actions such as a $7 billion share repurchase and a 21% dividend increase, and positive analyst sentiment. The company's optimistic outlook for 2026 and 2030, along with its dominant market share in process control, further contributed to investor confidence despite some valuation concerns. Technical indicators show a buy signal with a MACD of 13.05, while its P/E ratio of 44.35 suggests potential overvaluation.
